description: Lock military_engineer_corps to Vauban-era French royal engineer-corps scope, not generic ancient field engineers or later national engineer corps.

checks.reason: The source-backed scope is Vauban's corps de Genie, dated to 1690 in the cited Britannica history.
The scoped corps is a fortification-and-siegecraft engineer corps, so artillery and fortification instruction is the direct military-technical knowledge base.
Royal engineer work depended on plans, drawings, maps, offices, libraries, and archives for fortification and works coordination.
The Depot de la Guerre is a related military map/archive bureau, not a documented prerequisite for forming Vauban's engineer corps.
